Patch Set 4:

(1 comment)

It seems reasonable.

Note that for "lfs mirror resync" that this has very specific O_DIRECT semantics, because it is copying between two different versions of the file on the same inode, so it may require some effort to hook into copy_file_range().

commit.subject: LU-11621 utils: optimize lhsmtool_posix with copy_file_range()
commit.message:
LU-11621 utils: optimize lhsmtool_posix with copy_file_range()

Newer kernels and glibc offer copy_file_range() which avoids
a context switch needed with read() + write() for file data
copying. In the future Lustre can look to optimize this copy
on the server backend. Update lhsmtool_posix to use this new
functionality.

Change-Id: If63d57b89902f3d5c9ddde66901f3f55d15080f5
